steps.title: Identify the opening participial phrase
steps.content: The sentence opens with “Knowing that flying insects keep their backs pointed toward sunlight during the day,”—a participial phrase that must logically modify the subject of the main clause that follows.

steps.title: Apply the rule: opening participles attach to the subject
steps.content:
The subject must logically fit the phrase—it should be something that “knows” the fact about insects, not something that acts on instinct.

Only choice B makes “the researchers” the grammatical subject (“the researchers theorize that moths...”), so the opening phrase correctly attaches.

Choices A, C, and D either make “moths” the subject (illogical), displace the researchers awkwardly, or create unclear modification.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
